# MindNotes Pro honest outreach copy

Use these drafts when asking for real feedback. Keep the claims factual and update them when the product changes.

## Short positioning

MindNotes Pro is a local-first whiteboard that opens without an account and keeps your drawings in your browser.

It is not trying to replace Miro, tldraw, or Excalidraw for collaboration-heavy work. We are validating whether a lightweight, private, personal whiteboard is useful for quick diagrams, class notes, meeting sketches, and visual thinking.

Live demo: https://11suixing11.github.io/mindnotes-pro/
Feedback thread: https://github.com/11suixing11/mindnotes-pro/discussions/97
Source: https://github.com/11suixing11/mindnotes-pro

## Show HN / Indie Hackers

### Title

Show HN: MindNotes Pro — a local-first whiteboard with no account required

### Body

I am building MindNotes Pro, a small local-first whiteboard for quick sketches and diagrams.

What it does today:

- opens without signup
- stores drawings in your browser
- works as a PWA after first load
- supports pens, shapes, text, images, multiple documents, undo/redo, and PNG/PDF export
- is MIT licensed and self-hostable

What it is not:

- not a multiplayer collaboration tool
- not a replacement for professional design tools
- not a cross-device cloud notebook

I am trying to validate whether this is actually useful outside my own workflow. If you try it, I would especially like to know:

1. What did you try to use it for?
2. Did you understand the value in the first minute?
3. What made you hesitate or close it?
4. What is missing before you would use it again?
5. Would you choose it over Excalidraw, tldraw, Miro, or a notes app? Why or why not?

Live demo: https://11suixing11.github.io/mindnotes-pro/
Feedback thread: https://github.com/11suixing11/mindnotes-pro/discussions/97
Source: https://github.com/11suixing11/mindnotes-pro

Blunt feedback is welcome.

## Reddit / r/webdev

### Title

I built a local-first whiteboard and need honest product feedback

### Body

I have been building MindNotes Pro, a browser-based whiteboard focused on quick personal sketches rather than team collaboration.

The current direction:

- no account
- no server-side storage
- drawings stay in local browser storage
- offline-capable PWA
- pens, shapes, text, images, multi-document workspace, and export

I am not sure yet whether the product is actually useful, so I am looking for direct feedback rather than stars.

If you try it, please tell me:

- what you expected before opening it
- whether the first minute made sense
- what felt confusing or unnecessary
- what would make you come back tomorrow
- whether you would use this instead of Excalidraw, tldraw, Miro, Apple Notes, or nothing at all

Live demo: https://11suixing11.github.io/mindnotes-pro/
Feedback thread: https://github.com/11suixing11/mindnotes-pro/discussions/97
Source: https://github.com/11suixing11/mindnotes-pro

## Maintenance checklist before posting

- Verify the live demo opens and basic drawing works.
- Do not claim unmeasured bundle size or load time.
- Do not claim real users or testimonials unless they are public and attributed.
- Do not describe it as a Miro replacement.
- Ask for concrete feedback, not just stars.
